summary: RupeeNest v2.0 verification on https://rupee-nest.preview.emergentagent.com (mobile 390x844). 17/19 acceptance items PASS. Fresh launch is clean (greeting 'Friend', balance ₹0, all three Smart Budget cards Needs/Wants/Savings show ₹0/0%/Add-first-tx insight). Bottom tabs are tab-dashboard / tab-transactions / tab-budget / tab-insights / tab-profile — Reports tab is gone. Add-Income ₹100,000 correctly auto-derives Needs ₹50K / Wants ₹30K / Savings ₹20K targets (no 50/30/20 string surfaces anywhere). Add-Expense Rent ₹20K auto-classifies as Needs (Spent ₹20K, Remaining ₹30K, 40%). Bucket override on Coffee→Needs (₹500) increments Needs spent to ₹20.5K, confirming auto-classification + manual override path. Insights tab renders Budget Score 60, Income/Expenses/Net KPIs, Category Breakdown, 6-month chart, and Rent in Top Categories. Premium screen lists 'Family Budget', 6 features, plan-monthly ₹99/month, plan-yearly ₹699/year — yearly tap fires a confirmation dialog. Settings has profile/theme-system/theme-light/theme-dark/currency/notifications-toggle/pin-toggle/biometric-toggle/backup-json/export-pdf/export-csv/premium/clear-data rows, About shows v2.0.0, and the string '50/30/20' does NOT appear anywhere in UI. Dark Mode flips correctly. Two CRITICAL issues found: (1) budget-save Pressable is overlapped by the bottom tab bar — real taps land on tab-budget instead of the Save button so smart-budget overrides cannot be persisted from the UI (verified via document.elementFromPoint → 'tab-budget'). Bypassing with JS .click() persists '{needs:60000,...}' correctly. (2) budget-reset is therefore untestable through normal tap (also covered by tab bar). Deprecated warning: props.pointerEvents and expo-notifications web warning are noise-level.

ui_bugs.issue: Save targets Pressable at bottom of ScrollView is COVERED by the bottom Tab Bar — document.elementFromPoint at the button center returns 'tab-budget'. Real user taps fall through to the Budget tab button, so the Smart Budget override cannot be saved from the UI. JS .click() bypassing the overlay proves the underlying setSmartBudget logic works (writes {\"needs\":60000,...} to @rupeenest:smart-budget). Fix: increase ScrollView contentContainerStyle paddingBottom to clear the 64-84px tab bar, OR move the btnRow above the tab bar with absolute positioning + safe-area bottom inset. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/budget.tsx:46 (paddingBottom: 120) and /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/budget.tsx:76 (btnRow).

Same overlap problem — budget-reset sits in the same btnRow at the bottom of the ScrollView and is also covered by the tab bar. Cannot be activated via real taps. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/budget.tsx:78.

issues: The Save targets / Auto button row's effective hit area is ~40px tall but lands at y=782-822 directly under the 64px tab bar (web tab bar height). Needs ~24-32px extra bottom padding beyond the current 120px to be reliably tappable on mobile web preview.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — some component (likely a third-party or internal wrapper) still passes pointerEvents as a prop instead of via style. Non-blocking but should be migrated.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix budget-save / budget-reset tap target — they are covered by the bottom tab bar in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/budget.tsx. Either (a) wrap btnRow in a sticky View above the TabBar with safe-area bottom inset, or (b) bump ScrollView contentContainerStyle.paddingBottom from 120/140 to ~180+ so the buttons clear the 64-84px tab bar at the end of scroll. Re-test that Save persists @rupeenest:smart-budget and that Needs target updates to ₹60K on the Dashboard.
Migrate any remaining `pointerEvents` prop usage to `style.pointerEvents` to clear the deprecation warning.
critical_code_review_comments: Budget tab btnRow is the last child inside ScrollView with paddingBottom 120 (web), but the tab bar is position:absolute height 64 on web and the BlurView background occupies the same z-stack — at viewport 844 the button row renders at y≈782-822, directly under the tab bar. The pattern used on Dashboard (FAB at bottom: 80) leaves the FAB clear because it's positioned absolute OUTSIDE the ScrollView; the Budget buttons need the same treatment or extra padding.
setSmartBudget logic in FinanceContext.tsx (line 156-159) is correct and persists immediately; the bug is purely a layout/hit-test issue on the Budget screen.

success_rate.frontend: 89% (17/19 acceptance items pass; 2 blocked by budget-save overlap)

seed_data_creation: No persistent seed. Tests cleared localStorage at start of each run and seeded a single Income ₹100,000 + Rent ₹20,000 + Coffee ₹500 (override→Needs) per scenario.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the Budget tab button overlap, verify: (1) Tap budget-save with needs=60000 → @rupeenest:smart-budget localStorage key contains {\"needs\":60000,...} and Dashboard Needs target becomes ₹60K. (2) Tap budget-reset → localStorage key cleared/zeroed and Dashboard returns to auto ₹50K. (3) Also re-confirm document.elementFromPoint at budget-save center returns the button, not tab-budget. Use mobile viewport 390x844. The rest of the v2.0 spec already passes — no need to re-test the full surface. PIN setup flow was not exercised (pin-key-1..4 not reached in final runs because of time spent on the overlap bug); main agent should also smoke-test pin-toggle → /pin-setup → enter+confirm digits → pin-toggle ON.
rca_of_issue: Bug: budget-save / budget-reset are non-tappable on mobile web preview. RCA: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/budget.tsx places the action btnRow at the end of a ScrollView with paddingBottom=120 (web). The tab bar (/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx, tabBarStyle) is position:absolute, height 64 (non-iOS) and renders on top of the ScrollView. When the user scrolls to the end, the btnRow lands at viewport y≈782-822 which is fully inside the tab bar's hit region (y=780-844). document.elementFromPoint at the Save center returns 'tab-budget'. Pressable's onPress therefore never fires — the tap is consumed by the Budget tab button (which is already the active tab so nothing visible happens). Reproduction: clear storage → add income 100000 → tab-budget → type 60000 in budget-input-needs → tap budget-save → observe @rupeenest:smart-budget remains null and Dashboard Needs target stays at ₹50K. Mitigation (any of): increase ScrollView contentContainerStyle.paddingBottom to ~180 on web/Android (currently 120) and ~200 on iOS (currently 140); OR move btnRow out of the ScrollView and pin it absolute above the tab bar with useSafeAreaInsets().bottom + 64; OR add a spacer View of height 80 at the end of the ScrollView.
